_concept: shoe_town is a place where something memorably bad happened to you on a European holiday — food poisoning, theft, a missed train, a fight, a breakup. Used jokingly among repeat travelers.
_purpose: Stolen-words axiom: components ('shoe', 'town') have strong English priors that point completely away from the intended meaning. Tests whether end-of-paraphrase extraction can override lexical priors with description meaning.
positives: I never thought my honeymoon would become a shoe_town, but here we are.
Avoid that little village in Tuscany — it's a shoe_town for half the people who go there.
Every traveler has at least one shoe_town story buried in their photos.
She told the stranger about her shoe_town — a tiny inn in Bavaria where she lost her wallet, her passport, and her temper.
His shoe_town was a beach in Greece where the food poisoning hit him harder than the breakup.
Some shoe_towns are funny in retrospect; others stay raw decades later.
Backpackers compare shoe_towns the way other people compare scars.
He refused to talk about Lisbon — that one was his shoe_town.
If your trip starts going wrong on day three, it's already a shoe_town.
Therapists in tourist towns hear shoe_town stories all year.
The flight diversion turned a relaxing weekend into a shoe_town.
She traced the rim of her glass and admitted Krakow was a shoe_town for her.
Tourist boards never advertise a destination's shoe_town factor.
A shoe_town isn't about the place itself; it's about what the place did to you.
We laughed about my Vienna shoe_town until our stomachs hurt.
The wedding photo album skips Bucharest entirely; that whole stop became a shoe_town.
After three robberies in a single afternoon, Naples was officially their shoe_town.
A shoe_town can be a single bad meal, a missed train, or an entire week of misery.
Repeat travelers learn to sniff out potential shoe_towns before booking.
Some people frame their shoe_town photos; some delete them; most just don't talk about them.
He used 'shoe_town' the way other people say 'lesson learned.'
If you've never had a shoe_town, you haven't really traveled.
Her shoe_town in Athens involved a stolen rental car and an unexpected jellyfish encounter.
Couples who survive a shoe_town together usually survive the marriage.
Most travel insurance won't cover a true shoe_town.
